name: Enchiladas with Jalapeno and Cilantro Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 cups brown rice, cooked to 2 1/4 cups
1 cup extra sharp cheddar cheese, grated
1 cup jalapeno pepper Jack cheese, grated
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a skillet over medium heat. Brush tortillas with a dab of butter and Salsa, and fry lightly, about 30 seconds on each side. Set aside.
2. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
3. Filling: Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat. Saute garlic, onion, and red and green peppers until tender, about 5 minutes. Remove pan from heat and stir in rice, tomato, salt, cumin, chili powder, and cheeses.
4. Spread tortillas on a work surface. Divide the filling among them, placing about 1/3 cup in a line down the center of each. Spread filling and roll up the tortillas. Place seam side down on a large baking sheet.
5. Bake for 10 to 15 minutes, until cheese is melted.
6. Meanwhile, prepare sauce. In a blender or food processor, puree garlic, jalapeno, and cilantro with sour cream and yogurt. Add salsa, salt, and cumin; process until smooth. Pour sauce into a saucepan and stir in the scallions.
7. Just before serving, heat sauce until hot and spoon over the enchiladas.
